title: AOG daemon exposes unauthenticated plaintext Raft mutation RPCs because its shipped mTLS implementation is not integrated

affected_locations.label: composition
affected_locations.detail: default HTTP client and plain router are always selected

affected_locations.label: unused_control
affected_locations.detail: mutual-TLS configs exist but are not consumed

method: static end-to-end integration trace and call-site search
evidence: Daemon::app directly merges aog_wire::router at aogd/src/lib.rs:196-204
Raft handlers deserialize arbitrary bytes and call openraft at aog-wire/src/lib.rs:191-215
NodeTls server/client configs are not referenced by daemon code
loom-harness opts into non-loopback exposure with AOGD_ALLOW_INSECURE_BIND=1
proof_gap: A live forged Raft packet was not sent because the static source-to-state-mutation path is complete and destructive cluster testing is unnecessary for candidate validation.

facts: attacker reaches listener locally or on an opted-in network bind
attacker supplies a validly encoded Raft RPC
handler calls live Raft append/vote/snapshot methods
blocking_controls: non-loopback binding is refused by default unless AOGD_ALLOW_INSECURE_BIND=1
